mechanism_narrative: Low and stable inflation, as indicated by the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL), provides a favorable environment for corporate earnings and reduces uncertainty, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat during periods where CPIAUCSL is stable and below 2.5% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market and potential economic recession, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate profits decline over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances following a triggered increase in UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a stable federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates robust consumer demand without immediate monetary tightening pressure, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative when RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth and FEDFUNDS remains within a 25 basis point range over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates aggressive monetary tightening, which typically leads to lower equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25 percentage points in a month AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth is above 3.0%.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of instances where FEDFUNDS is rising during high inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Robust housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong construction activity and consumer confidence, which translates into broader economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST reports above 1500 thousand units (seasonally adjusted annual rate).
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of instances when HOUST exceeds 1500 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a strong and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth rate is greater than 0.1% month-over-month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat during periods of sustained PAYEMS growth, with a Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall economic activity, leading to reduced corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by more than 0.5% month-over-month for two consecutive months.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances following a triggered decline in INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining GDP (GDPC1) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a severe economic downturn, which will lead to significant declines in corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 contracts for two consecutive quarters AND UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 6 months.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 30% of instances when both GDPC1 contracts and UNRATE rises significantly.
